docs.title: SkillOS
docs.text: SkillOS is a backend-agnostic skill repository format built on fsspec. It lets you store, discover, and manage agent skills — locally, on S3, GCS, Azure, or any filesystem fsspec supports.

docs.title: Packages
docs.text: Package Description skillos-core Core abstractions: SkillRepo, Skill, License.

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
from skillos_core import SkillRepo

repo = SkillRepo("./my-skills")

# list all skills
for skill in repo:
    print(f"{skill.name}: {skill.description}")

# create a new skill
repo.insert(
    name="hello-world",
    description="A minimal example skill.",
    body="# Hello World\n\nThis skill does nothing yet.",
)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install skillos-core

Or with uv:

uv add skillos-core

docs.title: Create a skill repository
docs.text:
A skill repository is any directory (or remote path) where each subdirectory contains a SKILL.md file.

my-skills/
├── hello-world/
│   └── SKILL.md
└── code-review/
    ├── SKILL.md
    └── prompt.txt

docs.title: Open and read skills
docs.text:
from skillos_core import SkillRepo

repo = SkillRepo("./my-skills")

# list skill names
print(repo.list_skills())  # ['code-review', 'hello-world']

# read a specific skill
skill = repo.read("hello-world")
print(skill.description)
print(skill.body)

docs.title: Write skills
docs.text:
repo.insert(
    name="summarize",
    description="Summarize a document into bullet points.",
    body="# Summarize\n\nGiven a document, produce a concise summary.",
    license="MIT",
    allowed_tools=["Read", "Write"],
)

Update an existing skill:

repo.update("summarize", description="Summarize any document concisely.")

Delete a skill:

repo.delete("summarize")

docs.title: Remote backends
docs.text:
Because SkillOS uses fsspec, any supported protocol works:

# S3
repo = SkillRepo("s3://my-bucket/skills", anon=False)

# GCS
repo = SkillRepo("gs://my-bucket/skills")

# In-memory (useful for tests)
repo = SkillRepo("memory://test-repo")

docs.title: SkillRepo
docs.text:
from skillos_core import SkillRepo

A repository of skills backed by any fsspec filesystem.

docs.title: SkillRepo(url, **storage_options)
docs.text: Open a skill repository at the given URL. The protocol selects the backend — local paths, s3://, gs://, az://, memory://, etc. Extra keyword arguments are passed to fsspec.

docs.title: list_skills() -> list[str]
docs.text: Return sorted names of all skills (subdirectories containing a SKILL.md).

docs.title: read(name) -> Skill
docs.text: Read and parse a single skill by name. Raises FileNotFoundError if it doesn't exist.

docs.title: insert(name, description, body, *, license="MIT", allowed_tools=None, compatibility=None, metadata=None) -> Skill
docs.text:
Create a new skill. Validates name and description against the SKILL.md spec. Raises FileExistsError if the skill already exists.

Parameter Type Description name str Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens. Max 64 chars. description str 1–1024 chars, non-empty. body str Free-form markdown body. license License \| str SPDX identifier. Default MIT. allowed_tools list[str] \| None Tools the skill may use. compatibility str \| None Compatibility string. metadata dict \| None Arbitrary nested metadata.

docs.title: update(name, *, body=None, description=None, license=None, allowed_tools=None, compatibility=None, metadata=None) -> Skill
docs.text: Partial update of an existing skill. None means leave unchanged. Raises FileNotFoundError if the skill doesn't exist.

docs.title: delete(name) -> None
docs.text: Remove a skill and all its bundled resources. Raises FileNotFoundError if the skill doesn't exist.

docs.title: Iteration
docs.text:
SkillRepo supports iteration and containment checks:

for skill in repo:
    print(skill.name)

if "my-skill" in repo:
    print("found it")

docs.title: Skill
docs.text:
from skillos_core import Skill

A parsed skill. Returned by SkillRepo.read() and SkillRepo.insert().

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description name str The skill's directory name. metadata dict Parsed YAML frontmatter. body str Markdown body after frontmatter.

docs.title: description -> str | None
docs.text: The description field from frontmatter, if present.

docs.title: list_resources() -> list[str]
docs.text: Return sorted relative paths of all files in the skill directory, excluding SKILL.md itself.

docs.title: read_resource(path) -> bytes
docs.text: Read a bundled resource file by relative path.

docs.title: License
docs.text:
from skillos_core import License

Enum of common SPDX license identifiers. Accepts string coercion (License("MIT")).

Member Value MIT MIT APACHE_2_0 Apache-2.0 GPL_3_0 GPL-3.0 BSD_3_CLAUSE BSD-3-Clause GPL_2_0 GPL-2.0 BSD_2_CLAUSE BSD-2-Clause LGPL_3_0 LGPL-3.0 MPL_2_0 MPL-2.0 AGPL_3_0 AGPL-3.0 UNLICENSE Unlicense
